perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that any Confidential Information revealed to the other by the Disclosing Party remains the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party and its successors and assigns, unless otherwise expressly provided in writing signed by an authorized representative of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that any Confidential Information revealed to the other by the Disclosing Party becomes the joint property of both parties, unless otherwise expressly provided in writing signed by an authorized representative of both parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original ownership clause. Initially, Confidential Information remains the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party. The modified clause states that it becomes joint property, creating a fundamental conflict regarding ownership rights and potential disputes over its use and control.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership of Information. The parties agree that any Confidential Information revealed to the other by the Disclosing Party becomes the joint property of both parties, unless otherwise expressly provided in writing signed by an authorized representative of both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that confidential information becomes the joint property of both parties, whereas the original text stated it remains the exclusive property of the disclosing party. The contradicted text reinforces the changed text, indicating joint ownership. This directly creates a conflict regarding ownership rights as the two texts cannot coexist. The modified clause directly breaks compatibility with the prior version as well as with the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement. Any amendment or modification of this Agreement shall be in writing and executed by a duly authorized representative of the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the initial agreement between parties, subject to future unwritten or verbal agreements with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement. Further amendment or modification of this Agreement shall be done by the parties either in writing or verbal.
perturbation.explanation: The original text establishes that the current document is the final say with all the agreements held by the party. The modified clause states that further agreements could be verbal. This creates uncertainty around what agreement holds. As such, disputes could arise.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (e) Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the initial agreement between parties, subject to future unwritten or verbal agreements with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement. Further amendment or modification of this Agreement shall be done by the parties either in writing or verbal.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement is the entire agreement and all modifications must be in writing. The changed text says that it's the initial agreement and allows verbal agreements. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. The modified text creates a direct contradiction because it introduces the possibility of verbal agreements, whereas the original text specified written agreements only, now the agreement is no longer considered final and could be verbally changed. This is a clear inconsistency as per the definition.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information may be disclosed either visually, orally or in writing. Written material shall be identified and labeled "Confidential" or "Proprietary" to discloser. Verbal or visual information should be identified as "Confidential Information" when disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information may be disclosed either visually, orally or in writing. Written material need not to be identified and labeled "Confidential" or "Proprietary" to discloser. Verbal or visual information is not required to be identified as "Confidential Information" when disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that when information is being disclosed, it should be labeled as proprietary. By removing that requirement, it is difficult to identify what information is protected, causing the contract to not protect information properly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure of Proprietary Information. The parties agree that from the date of receipt, the party receiving the Confidential Information (hereinafter the "Receiving Party") shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other person, firm, corporation or other entity or use it for its own benefit except as provided in this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall not publish, divulge, communicate, or reveal any Confidential Information to any person, corporation, or other third party or to any of Receiving Party's employees who do not have a need to know such Confidential Information with respect to their job duties. The Receiving Party shall use the same degree of care to avoid publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it would with respect to its own confidential information. These efforts shall specifically include document control measures, such as numbered copies and sign out logs, and imposing on all employees, agents and other representatives of Receiving Party restrictions at least as strict as required by this Agreement. "Confidential Information" shall not include information, technical data or know-how which: (a) is already known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure and is not otherwise subject to restriction; (b) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party; (c) rightfully disclosed to Receiving Party by a third party who has no obligation of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party; (d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party; or (e) is approved for release by written authorization of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for written, verbal, and visual information to be identified as confidential when disclosed. This directly contradicts the section on 'Non-Disclosure of Proprietary Information,' which relies on the ability to identify 'Confidential Information' to enforce non-disclosure obligations. Without a clear method of identifying what information is confidential, the receiving party cannot reasonably be expected to protect it, creating a direct and significant contradiction, especially considering the agreement discusses numbered copies, sign-out logs, and restrictions based on job duties, all of which depend on easily identifying what information is confidential.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will be binding upon the successors and/or assigns of the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will not be binding upon the successors and/or assigns of the parties unless explicitly stated in a signed addendum.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the original text that the contract is binding upon successors, it may cause ambiguity in terms of what actually is binding. The clause introduces an in-text contradiction because the main agreement specifies the contract is binding, but the perturbation requires additional documentation. There could be some issues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Successors and Assigns. This Agreement will not be binding upon the successors and/or assigns of the parties unless explicitly stated in a signed addendum.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states the agreement *will* be binding on successors/assigns, while the changed text states it *will not* be binding unless explicitly stated in a signed addendum. The contradicted text mirrors the changed text, however, since the change introduces uncertainty about whether the agreement is binding or not, this causes a strong contradiction because the contract does not provide a clear method to determine when the contract will be binding, which may cause ambiguity and legal issues.
